steps.title: Understand what the blank must mean
steps.content:
The passage says Bell's tribute passage is a perfect ______ of Wentworth's style, such that anyone familiar with Wentworth would see the resemblance. The blank must name something that reproduces or closely copies an original—and fits the admiring context of a tribute.
  • Choice A — ‘forgetting’: forgetting means failing to remember; a tribute meant to pay homage would not be described as forgetting the style it admires. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘rejection’: rejection means dismissing as unworthy; Bell admired Wentworth, so rejecting his style contradicts the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘imitation’: imitation means a reproduction that closely copies something; a perfect imitation of Wentworth's style would naturally reveal the resemblance described. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘opinion’: opinion means a view or judgment; the sentence is not saying the passage expresses a view—it says the passage stylistically resembles Wentworth's writing. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only “imitation” describes something that reproduces another's style so closely that observers see the resemblance. That confirms C is correct.
